methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.5B - $7.5B.

private.name: Flock Safety
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series G), post-money $7.5B

data.narrative:
Shield AI, a Series G defense and security technology company, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 10 firms (2 public, 8 private) at similar or adjacent stages. The target's last post-money valuation stands at $5.6B following $1.5B+ in total capital raised.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage and sector. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement, implies $7.0B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Flock Safety ($7.5B, Series G), Skydio ($2.2B, Series F), and Destinus ($1.5B, late stage)—yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $7.5B. The discrepancy between methods reflects wide dispersion in the peer set: Flock Safety's $7.5B valuation pulls stage-progression higher, while Destinus and Skydio anchor the peer median lower.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $8.0B, with a median of $4.6B. Shield AI's current $5.6B valuation sits above the median, consistent with stage-progression but elevated relative to peer medians. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of venture-stage multiples in defense tech, the comparability of Flock Safety as a peer given its sector-leading position, and near-term pathway to revenue inflection relative to peers' growth trajectories.
